135 Avenue A_ _Avenue A, Where Legends Stay

MLA BIBLIOGRAPHY & LYRICAL PHRASE EXPLANATIONS


[16] "One-three-five Avenue A, where the borough breathes" → Anchored in the Oasis Map

confirmation of the address and construction era. Unknown Author. Oasis Map, p. 1.


[16] "Built in twenty-eight, brick and mortar underneath" → Directly drawn from: "Was built in

1928." Unknown Author. Oasis Map, p. 1.


[9] "Eighteen-fifty-three, stone stores lining the street" → Inspired by the 1853 Perris Maps description of "a Brick or stone store third class" and "a brick or stone dwelling first class (slate or metal roof & coped) with stores under." Perris. 1853 Perris Maps, p. 48.


[1] "Sixty-seven — not a structure, just the earth and concrete" → Directly from the Dripps

Plan: "on 139 Avenue A, there is reference to 0 structures." Dripps. 1867 Dripps Plan, p. 7.


[30] "Avenue A, where the legends stay / Lucy's been pouring since eighty-one, she ain't going away" → Drawn from: "Ludwika 'Lucy' Mickevicius, who has worked there since 1981." Holland. NYC Storefronts, p. 89.


[18] "Gold brick facade, sailor course at the crown" → Pulled from the architectural description: "Façade Color: Gold Fusion – 231 198 157, Façade Material: Brick, Roofline Description: Brick Parapet, Sailor Course." JG. Tompkins Square Park, p. 1.


[18] "Common bond, brick parapet — this is hallowed ground" → Drawn from: "Brickwork

Style: Common Bond, Roofline Description: Brick Parapet." JG. Tompkins Square Park, p. 1.


[3] "By eighty-five Robinson mapped six walls of brick" → Inspired by: "on 135 Avenue A, there is reference to 6 structures; a Brick Building, a Brick Building, a Brick Building, a Brick Building, a Brick Building, a Brick Building." Robinson. 1885 Robinson Atlas, p. 11.


[5] "Ninety-seven, five stories — watch this block get thick" → Pulled from: "on 135 Avenue A,

there is reference to 4 structures; a 5 story brick building." Bromley. 1897 Bromleys Atlas, p. 17.


[7] "Nineteen-thirty land book, stores below the stairs" → Drawn from: "on 135 Avenue A, there is reference to 4 structures; a 5 story Building with Store." Bromley. 1930 Land Book of the Borough of Manhattan, p. 38.

[12] "Fifty-five Manhattan, elevators in the air" → Inspired by: "5 Story Brick or Stone or

Concrete building with Store and Elevator." Bromley. 1955 Manhattan Land Book, p. 28.


[26] "Alt dot coffee, wireless buzzing free" → Drawn from: "Alt.coffee has free wireless access along with a good mug of cafe au lait." Chowhound INC. The Chowhound's Guide to the New York Tristate Area, p. 334.


[29] "Sustainable NYC — upcycling history" → Inspired by: "This is the East Village version of a general store, filled with 'local, organic, recycled, fair-trade, re-purposed, biodegradable products and gifts.'" Berman & Hederman. New York City With Children, p. 206.


[30] "Blanche retired, handed Lucy every key" → Drawn from: "the establishment legally still does business as Blanche's Bar—named after the original owner, who encouraged Lucy to take over after she retired." Holland. NYC Storefronts, p. 89.


[15] "From industrial school girls to this block's legacy" → Inspired by the history of Wilson's Industrial School for Girls: "The first industrial school established in this country was commenced some time in the year 1853... on the corner of Avenue A and St. Mark's place." Richmond. New York Institutions 1609-1872, p. 494.


[18] "Avenue A — brick parapet, sailor crown" → Again drawn from: "Roofline Description:

Brick Parapet, Sailor Course." JG. Tompkins Square Park, p. 1.


[16] "Twenty-eight built it, ain't never coming down" → Final callback to: "Was built in 1928."

Unknown Author. Oasis Map, p. 1.
